coaches.name: Jon Sumrall
coaches.title: Head Coach
coaches.bio: First-year head coach after leading Tulane to back-to-back AAC titles. Culture-driven leader installing a competitive standard across roster and staff. Emphasizes physicality, roster development through the portal, and defensive identity in partnership with Brad White.
highlights: Defensive identity partner with Brad White

coaches.name: Buster Faulkner
coaches.title: Offensive Coordinator
coaches.bio: Spread RPO architect from Georgia Tech. Builds rhythm passing, conflict reads, and vertical shots around Eric Singleton Jr. and a rebuilt offensive line. Installs pro-style structure with spread tempo elements.

coaches.name: Brad White
coaches.title: Defensive Coordinator
coaches.bio: 3-3-5 odd-front specialist returning from Kentucky. JACK and STAR roles define the defense — Jayden Woods and Kanye Clark are scheme centerpieces. Uses simulated pressures and hybrid personnel to match spread offenses.

coaches.name: Gerald Chatman
coaches.title: Assistant Head Coach / Defensive Line
coaches.bio: Promoted to assistant head coach while coaching the defensive line. Anchors the three-man front with Brendan Bett at nose and versatile edge pieces. Critical to run-fit integrity in the 3-3-5.

coaches.name: Joe Craddock
coaches.title: Quarterbacks Coach
coaches.bio: Tulane transfer who coached QBs under Sumrall. Develops Tramell Jones Jr. and Aaron Philo in the RPO rhythm offense. Focuses on pre-snap reads, conflict decisions, and protecting the football.

coaches.name: Chris Foster
coaches.title: Running Backs Coach
coaches.bio: Duke transfer coaching the running back room. Develops Jadan Baugh and the depth behind him in gap and zone schemes. Emphasizes pass protection and receiving skills for backs in spread sets.

coaches.name: Trent McKnight
coaches.title: Passing Game Coordinator / Inside Wide Receivers
coaches.bio: Passing game coordinator overseeing inside receivers and route concepts. Works with Buster Faulkner on route menu and spacing rules. Key to mesh, snag, and intermediate concepts in the offense.

coaches.name: Marcus Davis
coaches.title: Outside Wide Receivers Coach
coaches.bio: Auburn transfer coaching outside receivers including Eric Singleton Jr. Develops route running, separation, and vertical threat usage. Critical for boundary and slot-outside flexibility.

coaches.name: Evan McKissack
coaches.title: Tight Ends Coach
coaches.bio: Tulane transfer developing the tight end room in 12 and 11 personnel. Focuses on inline blocking, seam routes, and conflict leverage in the passing game.

coaches.name: Phil Trautwein
coaches.title: Offensive Line Coach
coaches.bio: Penn State transfer rebuilding the five-man front. Pass protection and run-game cohesion are swing factors for the 2026 offense. Integrates portal additions along the interior.

coaches.name: Greg Gasparato
coaches.title: Linebackers Coach
coaches.bio: Tulane transfer coaching inside linebackers in the 3-3-5. Run fits, blitz packages, and communication in nickel personnel are core responsibilities.

coaches.name: Bam Hardmon
coaches.title: Outside Linebackers Coach
coaches.bio: Tulane transfer coaching outside linebackers and edge roles in the odd front. Develops pass-rush plans and edge contain against spread run games.

coaches.name: Brandon Harris
coaches.title: Cornerbacks Coach
coaches.bio: UCF transfer coaching corners in a hybrid-heavy secondary. Critical for man and zone match rules against tempo offenses. Develops boundary corners and slot matchups.

coaches.name: Chris Collins
coaches.title: Safeties Coach
coaches.bio: Kentucky transfer coaching safeties and STAR/nickel roles. Develops deep-half players and hybrid defenders who fit the 3-3-5 structure.

coaches.name: Johnathan Galante
coaches.title: Special Teams Coordinator
coaches.bio: Tulane transfer leading special teams. Coverage units, return game, and field position are critical in close SEC games and rivalry week.

coaches.name: Rusty Whitt
coaches.title: Head Strength & Conditioning
coaches.bio: Tulane S&C coordinator setting the physical floor for Sumrall's culture. Fourth-quarter football and availability are program pillars.
